ddls:
CREATE TABLE "instances" (
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server instance */,
  "gcpolicy" TEXT /* Garbage collection policy used by the instance */,
  "cpu_min" INTEGER /* Minimum number of CPU cores assigned */,
  "cpu_max" INTEGER /* Maximum number of CPU cores assigned */,
  "mem_min" INTEGER /* Minimum memory size in MB */,
  "mem_max" INTEGER /* Maximum memory size in MB */,
  "cpu_req" INTEGER /* Requested number of CPU cores */,
  "mem_req" INTEGER /* Requested memory size in MB */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geographical location of the instance */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment type (e.g., prod, dev, staging) */,
  "instance_type" TEXT /* Type of instance (e.g., virtual machine, container) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instances_gcpolicy FOREIGN KEY ("gcpolicy") REFERENCES gc_policies ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "throughput_rate_3m" REAL /* Throughput rate over the last 3 minutes */,
  "response_time_rate_3m" REAL /* Response time rate over the last 3 minutes */,
  "max_response_time" REAL /* Maximum response time observed */,
  "response_time_50p" REAL /* 50th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_95p" REAL /* 95th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_97p" REAL /* 97th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_99p" REAL /* 99th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_99_9p" REAL /* 99.9th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_99_99p" REAL /* 99.99th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_99_999p" REAL /* 99.999th percentile response time */,
  "response_time_100p" REAL /* 100th percentile response time */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* Current CPU usage */,
  "mem_usage" REAL /* Current memory usage */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Current disk usage */,
  "network_io" REAL /* Current network I/O usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance metric entry */,
  "throughput_prom_ci" REAL /* Predicted throughput confidence interval */,
  "rsptime_prom_ci" REAL /* Predicted response time confidence interval */,
  "throughput_wrk" REAL /* Predicted throughput based on workload */,
  "responsetime_wrk" REAL /* Predicted response time based on workload */,
  "responsetime_max_wrk" REAL /* Predicted maximum response time based on workload */,
  "responsetime_stdev_wrk" REAL /* Standard deviation of predicted response time based on workload */,
  "web_errors" INTEGER /* Number of web server errors */,
  "thrpt_wrk_ci" REAL /* Confidence interval for predicted throughput based on workload */,
  "rsptime_wrk_ci" REAL /* Confidence interval for predicted response time based on workload */,
  "anomaly_score" REAL /* Anomaly score indicating the likelihood of an outlier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_predictions_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to (e.g., IT, DevOps, engineering) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  "accessed_resource" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., instance, metric, prediction) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance metric entry */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., CPU usage, memory usage) */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value that triggered the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was triggered */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gc_policies" (
  "policy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each garbage collection policy */,
  "policy_name" TEXT /* Name of the garbage collection policy */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the garbage collection policy */,
  "parameters" TEXT /* Policy-specific parameters (e.g., heap size, collection frequency) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_requirements" (
  "requirement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource requirement */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores required */,
  "memory_size" INTEGER /* Memory size in MB required */,
  "disk_space" INTEGER /* Disk space in GB required */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("requirement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_requirements_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "workloads" (
  "workload_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each workload */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "workload_type" TEXT /* Type of workload */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the workload */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the workload */,
  "workload_size" INTEGER /* Size of the workload (e.g., number of requests) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("workload_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_workloads_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log entry */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "error_type" TEXT /* Type of error */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  "error_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the error (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "capacity_planning" (
  "plan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each capacity planning entry */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "forecasted_load" REAL /* Forecasted workload load */,
  "recommended_resources" TEXT /* Recommended resources based on the forecast */,
  "planning_date" TEXT /* Date when the planning was done */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval for the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plan_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_capacity_planning_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log entry */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance activity */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the maintenance activity */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the maintenance activity */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes about the maintenance activity */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the maintenance activity (e.g., success, failure) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., alert, report) */,
  "email_notifications" INTEGER /* Whether to send email notifications */,
  "sms_notifications" INTEGER /* Whether to send SMS notifications */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server instance */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Report data in JSON format */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES instances ("instance_id")
)
